method.probes: two fresh-context Claude Code sessions, no prior conversation state, entry point CLAUDE.md only, allowed one hop through files CLAUDE.md references
method.grading: independent grader agent, author != subject (Level 0 invariant 0.1), rubric anchored to repo files

questions.Q1: What is NOOS, and name two things it explicitly must NOT own.
questions.Q2: Where does the canonical FOUNDER_CANON text live now, and what role does the FOUNDER_CANON_INTERFACE doc play relative to it?
questions.Q3: What must happen before you edit shared paths in this repo, and what command does it?
questions.Q4: Describe the motor in one sentence (clock to executor), and state whether you may redesign it.
questions.Q5: What law governs merge-conflict resolution here, and name two actions it forbids.
questions.Q6: Give the commit pin for the Master SSOT repo and the SG Library version string.
questions.Q7: Who may write PASS/DONE verdicts, and what phrase must a builder use instead?
questions.Q8: What gate applies before any broad repo read, and which file do you read first to satisfy it?

answers.answer: NOOS (noetfeld-OS) is the Noetfield execution and integration control plane: it turns governance canon into running loops, lanes, routing, and receipts, and integrates the Noetfield-Systems repos through registries and receipts. Two things it explicitly must NOT own: (1) product definition — SG canon (sina-governance-SSOT + SG Library); (2) buyer UX / website source — Noetfield.com. (Others listed: delivery implementation in SourceA, TrustField architecture/deploy/compliance/messaging, and founder decisions.)

The canonical FOUNDER_CANON text now lives in the SG library at P1-CANON/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md (v1.0, machine_wired), committed to the sina-governance-SSOT repo in commit 6c13aa27 (2026-07-07); the Desktop file 'FOUNDER_CANON (1).md' is a stale pre-commit draft not to be cited. docs/_NOOS_AGENT/[NOOS-AGENT-20260703-005]_FOUNDER_CANON_INTERFACE_v1.md is preserved as the NOOS operational binding of FOUNDER_CANON — an interface, not independent doctrine. Its old clause pointing upstream at the Desktop draft has been satisfied (the commit condition was met 2026-07-07), so upstream authority is the SG library copy; the interface doc's text is preserved unmodified, and its operative strings remain NOOS law verbatim (the dispatch line 'LAWS: FOUNDER_CANON v1 + governed-autorun v3. Violations = BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.' and the receipt stamp 'canon_version: FOUNDER_CANON_v1+MACHINE_LOOPS_v1').

Claim before edit (L-P5): before mutating shared paths you must claim a lane with `make local-lane TASK=NOOS-LANE-<id> SCOPE=path1,path2` (equivalently `AGENT_ID=claude-code-noos IDE=claude-code bash scripts/noos_local_claim_lane_v1.sh <task-id> <paths...>`). Also run `python3 scripts/noos_agent_conflict_check_v1.py --json` before parallel work, heartbeat sessions >20 min with `make local-heartbeat TASK=<id>`, and close out with `make local-closeout TASK=<id>` — never leave stale claims (L-P7).

One clock — the Cloudflare cron worker noos-loop-fleet-tick-v1 (*/5, iterating a 14-target dispatch table) — POSTs one bounded, secret-gated tick (X-NOOS-Loop-Secret) to one swappable HTTP executor, the Railway noos-loop-runner (ops/railway/noos-loop-runner/server.py, POST /loop running scripts/noos_loop_runner_v1.py), with receipts at exit and an independent dead-man (noos-deadman-v1, */30) watching liveness via Supabase noos_loop_registry. No — I may NOT redesign it: the motor is not redesigned, rebuilt, or 'improved' from a chat session; wiring is LOCKED in data/noos-motor-executor-wiring-v1.json (LOCKED_v1). Map it, verify it, never rebuild it.

Merge-conflict resolution is governed by the LOCKED PR-conflict law: before touching any conflict you must load .cursor/skills/pr-conflict-resolver/SKILL.md, classify every conflicted file as registry | receipt | LOCKED | generated | code, validate with `python3 scripts/verify_pr_conflict_resolution_v1.py --json`, and write a receipt (receipts/proof/noos-pr-conflict-resolution-<UTC>.json). Two forbidden actions: (1) blind `git checkout --ours`/`--theirs` on data/*.json, receipts/proof/, or *_LOCKED.md; (2) committing conflict markers. (It also mandates STOP on duplicate-ownership or LOCKED canon.)

Master SSOT (sina-governance-SSOT) commit pin: c280ff7e (full: c280ff7ebf548ff3a1784502a46436fa0e7bf9c0, main, read 2026-07-10). SG Library version string: v0.9-SG-RATIFIED (ratified 2026-07-05, merged 2026-07-06).

PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED/DONE verdicts may be written only by deterministic gates or independent verifiers (author ≠ subject) — never by the builder itself. A builder must instead write the phrase 'SUBMITTED for independent verification'. Additionally, no DONE claim is valid without a receipt_id, and absence of a receipt = FAIL.

The L0 broad-read gate: before ANY broad read of the repo you must first read graph-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md, then query targeted terms via `python3 scripts/query_repo_graph_v1.py <term>` — never blind-scan the repo.

NOOS (noetfeld-OS) is the Noetfield execution and integration control plane: the DELIVERY-lane runtime-control repo that turns governance canon into running loops, lanes, routing, and receipts, and integrates the Noetfield-Systems repos through registries and receipts. Two things it explicitly must NOT own (per CLAUDE.md and SYSTEM_IDENTITY.md): (1) product definition (SG canon — sina-governance-SSOT + SG Library), and (2) buyer UX / website source (Noetfield.com, `must_not_own: noetfield_website_source`). (Also listed: delivery implementation/SourceA, TrustField architecture/deploy/compliance/messaging, and founder decisions.)

The canonical FOUNDER_CANON text now lives in the SG library at `P1-CANON/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md` (v1.0, machine_wired), committed to the sina-governance-SSOT repo in commit 6c13aa2765dbb129bc4981da4f25ef1a8a96b84d (2026-07-07); the Desktop file `FOUNDER_CANON (1).md` is a stale pre-commit draft and must not be cited as authority. `docs/_NOOS_AGENT/[NOOS-AGENT-20260703-005]_FOUNDER_CANON_INTERFACE_v1.md` is preserved as the NOOS operational binding of FOUNDER_CANON — an interface, not independent doctrine. Its old clause naming the Desktop draft as upstream authority has been satisfied by the 2026-07-07 commit, so upstream authority is now the SG library copy; the interface doc's operative strings remain NOOS law verbatim (the dispatch line `LAWS: FOUNDER_CANON v1 + governed-autorun v3. Violations = BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.` and receipt stamp `canon_version: FOUNDER_CANON_v1+MACHINE_LOOPS_v1`), plus its intent filter, authority chain, failure routing, and three founder touchpoints.

Claim before edit (rule L-P5): before mutating shared paths you must claim a lane. Command: `make local-lane TASK=NOOS-LANE-<id> SCOPE=path1,path2` (equivalently: `AGENT_ID=claude-code-noos IDE=claude-code bash scripts/noos_local_claim_lane_v1.sh <task-id> <paths...>`). Additionally, run `python3 scripts/noos_agent_conflict_check_v1.py --json` before parallel work (exit 1 means another non-stale agent owns the scope), heartbeat sessions over 20 min with `make local-heartbeat TASK=<id>`, and close out with `make local-closeout TASK=<id>` (L-P7 — never leave stale claims).

The motor: one clock — the Cloudflare cron worker `noos-loop-fleet-tick-v1` (*/5, iterating a 14-target dispatch table) — POSTs one bounded tick to one swappable, secret-gated HTTP executor (`X-NOOS-Loop-Secret` → the canonical Railway `noos-loop-runner` at `POST /loop`, running `scripts/noos_loop_runner_v1.py`), with receipts written at exit and an independent dead-man worker (`noos-deadman-v1`, */30) watching liveness in the Supabase `noos_loop_registry` sink. No — I may not redesign it: the wiring is LOCKED (`data/noos-motor-executor-wiring-v1.json`, LOCKED_v1); the law is 'map it, verify it, never rebuild it' — the motor is not redesigned, not rebuilt, not "improved" from a chat session.

Merge-conflict resolution is governed by MANDATORY LOCKED law: the pr-conflict-resolver skill at `.cursor/skills/pr-conflict-resolver/SKILL.md` (locked via `data/noos-pr-conflict-skill-lock-v1.json`; law doc `[NOOS-AGENT-20260708-001]_PR_CONFLICT_RESOLVER_MANDATORY_v1_LOCKED.md`), which must be loaded before touching any conflict; every conflicted file must be classified as registry | receipt | LOCKED | generated | code, resolution validated with `python3 scripts/verify_pr_conflict_resolution_v1.py --json`, and a receipt written. Two forbidden actions: (1) blind `git checkout --ours`/`--theirs` on `data/*.json`, `receipts/proof/`, or `*_LOCKED.md` files; (2) committing conflict markers (also forbidden: proceeding past duplicate-ownership or LOCKED-canon conflicts instead of STOPping).

Master SSOT repo (sina-governance-SSOT) commit pin: `c280ff7e` (full: `c280ff7ebf548ff3a1784502a46436fa0e7bf9c0`, main, read 2026-07-10). SG Library version string: `v0.9-SG-RATIFIED` (ratified 2026-07-05, merged 2026-07-06).

PASS / FAIL / BLOCKED / DONE verdicts may only come from deterministic gates or independent verifiers (author ≠ subject) — never from the builder itself. A builder must instead write exactly: "SUBMITTED for independent verification". Additionally, no DONE claim is valid without a receipt_id, and absence of a receipt = FAIL.

The L0 broad-read gate (L0 repo graph memory) applies before any broad repo read: never blind-scan the repo or spawn a broad 'understand the repo'/'map subsystem'/'audit' pass by reading files directly. The file to read first is `graph-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md` (the compact subsystem map), followed for anything it doesn't answer by `python3 scripts/query_repo_graph_v1.py <term>`; only then open the specific files the graph names. If the graph is missing or stale, rebuild it first with `python3 scripts/build_repo_graph_v1.py`.
